instruction: I need you to write me an email to my wife's sister Sarah. The email is to let her know about our dinner next Monday at 7. The email must be written exclusively in English and include an English idiom. It should consist of at least six sentences, with the first sentence starting with the phrase "Dear Sarah" and the last sentence ending with the phrase "looking forward to it." The keyword "dinner" must appear exactly twice in the email. The tone should be warm and conversational, written in the first person, and include a brief mention of why the dinner is special. The last sentence must end with the phrase "Thanks for your time."
llm_response: Dear Sarah, I hope this email finds you well and that you are having a wonderful week. I am writing to confirm that our dinner next Monday at 7 is all set, and it will be a truly special occasion because we are celebrating a milestone in our lives. Please know that this dinner is not just a meal but a chance for us to share some cherished memories with you. We have already ordered the menu, and I am so excited to see what everyone brings to the table. I can't wait to catch up with you and make this dinner a memorable one. Thanks for your time.
evaluation_result:
**Analysis**: The instruction contains a direct conflict regarding the ending of the email: one constraint requires the last sentence to end with "looking forward to it," while another constraint requires the last sentence to end with "Thanks for your time." The model failed to recognize this conflict. Instead, it provided a response that followed the "Thanks for your time" constraint but ignored the "looking forward to it" constraint. It also failed to include an English idiom as requested. Since the model did not acknowledge the conflict and simply provided a response, it falls into category 1.

**Answer**: 1

Conflicts between Phrase Constraints.new_constraint: The last sentence must end with the phrase "Thanks for your time."
Conflicts between Phrase Constraints.org_constraint: The last sentence must end with the phrase "looking forward to it."
